reviews.review_msg: Cumulative final tested on 15 readings and all lectures. When asked for guidance, she says you need to know everything. A cumulative in-person exam not feasible for a 6-week intersession class. Take-home final would have been much more appropriate, especially for an intersession class.

Great prof, very accessible and TA's were very helpful. She doesn't record her lectures so showing up is really important. Only thing is she speaks very fast and it's hard to catch all the information sometimes, but nothing that can't be clarified with her or a TA. She was lenient with extensions and overall really good intro class.
Very organized and insightful lectures. This class is easy if you actually study (lol)

She is a nice professor who wants all her students to succeed. Something that I find challenging is her lectures. Her slides tend to be vague. She elaborates on them throughout the lecture, but she tends to talk very fast, so it is hard to understand or even take note of certain concepts. Her tutorials were useful. The final written part was hard.

Dr. Butler is easy to talk to, and her assignments are clear/reasonable. However, Dr. Butler's slides hold little to no content, so it's important to focus on whatever she says and ask her to repeat if needed. At times, it felt as if she were rapping the lectures. Not much test review in-class/ short answer was challenging.

CRIM 101, not 131. her lecture content was very similar to the textbook (which is Free), but what she says during lectures shows up on exams. Midterm was fairly easy as it was just mc, but final was more difficult with the short answers. Know video examples shown in class esp for midterm. Very approachable and always willing to help. Fairly easy

(this is actually for CRIM 101) Dr. Butler was great, very transparent on assignment guidelines and grading. Lectures are well organized and most of it is already in the textbook provided, lectures mostly help with examples and clarification. She's very friendly, would encourage questions and discussion but wouldn't say is mandatory.

Her lectures are very easy to follow and pretty engaging. The exams are very straightforward as long as you understand the main concepts from the chapters/lectures. There are various quizzes throughout the semester that are online and open-book to make sure you do the reading. I would take another class with Dr. Butler and recommend her classes!

Amanda was a very good professor for a first year course. Her lectures were straight forward and kept the students engaged, as the slides were open-ended and required additional notes. The tests were a difficult at times, but the online component of the course provided easy marks.
Very good at teaching and is very clear. Exams are pretty easy as long as you read the textbook!
